Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (medical assistance in dying â protection against coercion)
Identification.RunningHead: Preventing Coercion of Persons Not Seeking Medical Assistance in Dying Act

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to make it an offence for certain persons in certain circumstances to initiate a discussion with a person about the availability to them of medical assistance in dying, knowing that they have not specifically requested to have such a discussion.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Preventing Coercion of Persons Not Seeking Medical Assistance in Dying Act.
The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 241.3:

Section.MarginalNote: Unsolicited discussion â government officer or employee
Section.Text: Every person who, being an officer or employee of the Government of Canada or of a province, other than a medical practitioner or a registered nurse, including a nurse practitioner, initiates a discussion with a person about the availability to that person of medical assistance in dying is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction if they

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by virtue of their profession, are in a position of trust or authority towards that person; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: know that the person has not specifically requested to have such a discussion with them.
